Klar: nennt Anbieter, BERUFENET, Umfang (3500+) und Geographie. Es fehlt dedizierter whenToUse-Block mit Use-Cases.
Beide Tools haben klare Beschreibungen, sinnvolle Validierungsregeln, realistische Tests. Verknuepfung zwischen searchProfessions und getProfessionDetail explizit.
Beschreibung konkret: 183 Wettbewerbe weltweit, mit konkreten Datentypen (matches, standings, teams, scorers, players). Praezise und numerisch belegt.

Tool-Beschreibungen klar, Tests zeigen Code-Beispiele (BL1, PL). Auto-generierte Output-Doku ist generisch ('Status or error code', 'Tla value'), competitionId-Codes wie BL1/PL nicht in Parameter erlaeutert.
Clear scope: low-level blockchain data via Moralis (blocks, transactions, native history) across 30+ EVM chains. Good differentiation from other Moralis APIs.

Chain enum is parameterized (evmChains:alias) — useful. Tool keys are raw paths (/block/:block_number_or_hash) rather than semantic names, which is unhelpful for LLM routing. Descriptions are brief, truncated test descriptions, and no output schemas. Parameter constraints exist but are minimal.
Description names provider (Moralis), data type (OHLCV candlesticks), scope (EVM token pairs), and notable feature (recursive auto-pagination). Could mention key requirement explicitly.

Parameter list has rich enums (timeframe, fromDateUnit, currency) and a realistic WETH/USDC test. Weaknesses: chain enum uses unresolved placeholder syntax `{{evmChains:alias}}` which is opaque to an external LLM; no output schema; combined option string `optional(), default(1000)` is malformed.
Specific: wallet intelligence with concrete features (balance, history, net worth, profitability, active chain detection, ENS/Unstoppable resolution). Clear scope.

Chain enum is parameterized, boolean enums used. Tool keys are raw paths. No output schemas. Descriptions are brief but adequate. Truncated test descriptions. Good cross-reference to chain parameter and pagination cursors.
Beschreibung praezise: kulturelles Erbe aus europaeischen Museen, Bibliotheken, Archiven, Galerien ueber Europeana API. Klar und thematisch fokussiert.

Profile-Enum gut, aber 'qf' (Solr filter query) hat keine description-Erlaeuterung, kein Hinweis auf Syntax wie TYPE:IMAGE. Tool-Beschreibungen klar, aber parameter-level descriptions fehlen weitgehend.
Beschreibung klar: Google Fact Check Tools API, Text+Image Suche, ClaimReviews mit Ratings und Quellen. Anwendungsfall offensichtlich.

Cross-References gut, Tests mit echten Beispielen (politifact.com). Aber Output-Doku ist generisch ('TextualRating value', 'ClaimDate value' statt erlaeuternd), maxAgeDays/languageCode ohne Beispiel-Werte. listClaimReviewMarkup erwaehnt OAuth ohne weitere Details.

Specific: ERC20 token analytics via Moralis with many concrete features named (metadata, holders, transfers, DEX swaps, sniper detection, liquidity, balances, approvals, rankings). Good differentiation.

Chain and boolean enums parameterized. Tool keys are raw paths; multiple endpoints (e.g. /wallets/:address/tokens vs /:address/erc20) overlap in purpose without clear differentiation. No output schemas, sparse descriptions, generic test addresses.

Die Beschreibung nennt den Anbieter (German Federal Employment Agency), den Inhalt (apprenticeship and training offers), die Region (Germany) und erwaehnt Filteroptionen. Es fehlen jedoch konkrete Use-Case-Hinweise und Abgrenzungen zu aehnlichen Tools, weshalb sie zwar verstaendlich, aber nicht exemplarisch spezifisch ist.

Parameter sind klar typisiert mit min/max/default-Constraints und enthalten praezise Beschreibungen (z.B. 'Page number for pagination, starting at 0', Beispielwerte 'informatik, medizin, jura'). Der Hinweis zur Authentifizierungs-Blockierung von searchStudyPrograms ist nuetzlich; allerdings fehlen Details zum Response-Datumsformat und zur genauen Bedeutung von 'spipiinaktiv=0', was eine 5.0 verhindert.
Description clearly identifies the data domain (Berlin events: markets, festivals, public assemblies) and is specific enough to differentiate from other event sources.

Tools have no parameters (all GET-all endpoints), so parameter clarity is trivially satisfied. Tool descriptions are clear but minimal; output schema fields use German keys which may slightly confuse LLM downstream usage.
Benennt klar Quelle (Berlin Vergabeplattform), Format (RSS feed), Inhalt. Es fehlt expliziter whenToUse-Hinweis, Aktualisierungsfrequenz, Geltungsbereich.
Tool hat keine Parameter, korrekt gehandhabt. Tool-Beschreibung nennt Rueckgabefelder. LLM kann Aufruf eindeutig durchfuehren. Hinweis auf fehlende Filter waere hilfreich.
Description names provider (LiFi) and core capabilities (bridge quotes, chains, tools, transfer data). Specific to cross-chain bridging domain, clearly triggerable.

Parameters lack description fields, but test examples are comprehensive (chain IDs, USDC contract, real wallet addresses). Length constraints on txHash (66) and address (42) help. Missing format guidance for chain IDs as strings.
Description names institution (Bayerische Staatsbibliothek MDZ), volume (3.16M+ items), protocol (IIIF), and content categories (manuscripts, prints, maps, music). Very specific.

Parameters lack description fields. ID format inferred only from test examples (bsb10000001 pattern). Page number, image ID, and cursor lack format guidance. Tool descriptions are useful but parameter-level docs are absent.
Description identifies provider (TradingView) and output (chart screenshots), but is brief and lacks specific use cases (e.g., stock vs crypto, technical analysis). Acceptable but not exemplary.
Parameters lack description fields, but enums are human-readable (1m,3m,...,1M, light/dark, regular/extended), width/height constrained, sensible defaults, symbol format shown via test (NASDAQ:TSLA).
Sehr knappe Beschreibung listet die Endpunkte auf, nennt aber keinen konkreten Use-Case und gibt wenig Kontext zum Inhalt.
Alle Parameter mit Beschreibungen versehen (7 Tools, durchgaengig dokumentiert); Tool-Descriptions klar; gute Abdeckung der CoinGecko-Coins-Endpunkte.
Clear scope (DOI metadata) and concrete numbers (60M+ DOIs, 3000+ repositories); identifies content types.

Resource-type and sort enums are explicit, page[size] constraints set; however parameters lack per-parameter description fields, leaving query/doiPrefix/doiSuffix semantics implicit beyond tool description.
Mentions DeFi Llama and 'batch query ERC20 and native token prices' but actual schema only exposes single-token coingecko price lookups, slight overstatement.
Both insert params have descriptions with examples (ethereum, bitcoin, solana); enum restricts source to coingecko; tool description however is generic ('returns structured JSON').
Specific source (DESTATIS GENESIS) and resource types (tables, statistics, variables, metadata); concise.
Tool descriptions include selection pattern examples ('12411*'), constraints set; per-parameter descriptions missing, and auth requirement note is helpful but not enforced.
Clear specific scope (Drucksachen, Plenarprotokolle) with German terminology preserved; identifies source DIP API and parliament.

Enums for f.zuordnung (BT/BR/BV/EK) explicit; tool descriptions list filter options; per-parameter descriptions missing; filter prefixes (f.wahlperiode etc.) explained in tool description rather than parameter level.

Clear description naming the DIP API, listing the four resource types (Vorgaenge, Vorgangspositionen, Aktivitaeten, Personen) with German+English glosses. Could mention typical use cases or filters more explicitly.

Tool descriptions are clear and mention filter dimensions and cross-references between tools. However, individual filter parameters lack explicit per-parameter descriptions (e.g. expected values for f.wahlperiode, f.vorgangstyp, beratungsstand) which an LLM would need to call correctly.
Clear description listing functions (definitions, frequencies, IPA, snippets) and naming the DWDS source. Specific and actionable.

Tool descriptions are good (pipe-separator hint, max length, supported levels A1/A2/B1). However, individual parameter descriptions are missing, output schemas are generic ({result: object}), so LLM only has tool-level guidance.
Beschreibung klar: FamilySearch Places API fuer genealogische Ortsdaten, Jurisdiktionen, Place-Types. Fokus auf historische und moderne Locations gut erkennbar.

Cross-References vorhanden, aber Parameter sind ueber URL-Templates wie 'name:{{PLACE_NAME}}~' versteckt. Keine separate description pro Parameter, Lucene-Syntax (fuzzy ~, parentId:, distance:) wird nicht explizit erklaert.
Klare Beschreibung der Datenquelle (Irys GraphQL, decentralized storage), nennt zentrale Filter (owner, token, tags, timestamp) und auth requirements. Etwas knapp, aber spezifisch genug.

Tool descriptions sind klar mit Token-Typen und Rueckgabefeldern, aber Parameter haben keine individuellen description-Felder. Enum (ASC,DESC) und defaults helfen, doch Felder wie tagName/tagValue/after koennten mehr Kontext gebrauchen.

Beschreibt klar Provider (German IT procurement RSS) und Kategorien (software/hardware/internet/telecom/consulting), aber keine Hinweise auf Sprache der Inhalte, Aktualisierungsfrequenz oder Anwendungsfaelle.
Alle Tools sind parameterlos und gut beschrieben mit klaren Kategorien. Da es keine Parameter zum dokumentieren gibt, ist die Klarheit hoch — LLM kann sicher den richtigen Endpoint waehlen.
Klare Beschreibung der Datentypen (artist/track/album/charts/recommendations) und Quelle (Last.fm/listening data). Knapp, aber ausreichend spezifisch.

Tool descriptions sind gut, Tests mit realistischen Beispielen. Aber Parameter haben keine individuellen description-Felder. Max-Limits und Defaults sind okay, Output-Schemas sind oberflaechlich (oft type:object ohne nested properties).
Klare Beschreibung (LUKSO via BlockScout) mit Aufzaehlung der Datentypen (transactions, transfers, internal, logs, balances, history). Knapp, aber praezise.

Chain-enum klar (LUKSO_MAINNET/TESTNET), eine Validation (regex address). Aber 8 Tools nutzen nur knappe Boilerplate-Descriptions ('query by address hash'), keine individuellen Parameter-descriptions, Output-Schemas oft items:array of string (nicht structured).
Klare Beschreibung der vier Block-Funktionen (list/details/transactions/withdrawals) mit Quelle (LUKSO BlockScout). Spezifisch und kompakt.

Tool description fuer getBlocks erwaehnt 'type filters' aber keine zulaessigen Werte (uncle ist nur im Test erkennbar). chain-enum sauber. Output-Schemas teilweise detailliert (getBlocks), teilweise sehr shallow (getBlockTransactions). Keine individuellen Parameter-descriptions.
Klar abgegrenzt: NFT-Daten auf LUKSO (collections, instances, holders, transfers). Passt zum Schema-Inhalt, Quelle (BlockScout) genannt.

chain-enum klar, Tool-descriptions knapp aber treffend. getNFTInstanceById hat detailliertes Output-Schema mit LSP4Metadata-Struktur. Keine individuellen Parameter-descriptions, Adressformat nicht validiert (regex fehlt).
Klare Auflistung der Token-Funktionen (list, details, transfer history, holders, counters). Passt zu den fuenf Tools.

Output-Schemas fuer listTokens und getTokenByAddress detailliert mit allen Token-Feldern. Tool-Descriptions knapp aber treffend. Keine individuellen Parameter-descriptions, keine Adress-Regex-Validation.

Description is clear and specific — names the institution (BfG, German Federal Institute of Hydrology), the domain (marine environment), and the data source (coastal states and research institutions). Strong signal for when an LLM should select this schema.

Tools have descriptive names and clear descriptions. However, all 3 tools have empty parameters arrays — they take no inputs (parameterless POST requests). Output schemas are very well documented with per-field descriptions. Overall the schema enables correct tool invocation but the dimension is partially N/A since there are no parameters to evaluate.

Schema description is clear and specific about searching/retrieving movie, TV series, and episode information with examples of returned fields (ratings, plot, cast, poster). Slightly generic but adequate.

Tool descriptions are clear with cryptic OMDb-native keys (s, t, i, y). Enums for type/plot are well-defined and tests illustrate usage. Single-letter keys without expanded descriptions slightly reduce LLM clarity.
Description clearly identifies Pinata as IPFS gateway, access pattern (by CID), and auth requirement. Concise and specific.
Simple schema — one parameter (cid) without explicit description. Tests demonstrate real CIDs. The free_read_example tool is just a static example, which limits usefulness.

Description identifies Polymarket Gamma API and capabilities (search events, list events, list markets) but is brief and the 'readable, table-like summaries' phrasing is implementation detail rather than user-facing scope.

Many query params with explicit regex/enum constraints and defaults. Tests demonstrate diverse filter combos. However, dozens of params lack inline descriptions, requiring LLM to infer purpose from key names.
Description names ProfileJump for LUKSO Universal Profiles and lists capabilities (token prices, trending profiles, listings, profile lookup). Clear scope.

Most tools have no parameters or single enum/address. profileByAddress uses regex for Ethereum address format. Hardcoded pagination in path (?limit=100&offset=0) is rigid. Tool descriptions are clear with chaining hints.
Clear scope (Gnosis Safe multisig wallets) with key data dimensions (balances, transactions, owners, modules) and chain context (Ethereum mainnet). Concise.
Address validation via length(42), good pagination (limit min/max), cross-references between tools. getIncomingTransfers description has minor filler text. Tests use real Safe address.
Clear activity types listed (transfers, swaps, mints, burns, approvals) and scope (EVM chains, realtime). Decoded format noted.

Address regex validation correct. Chain list in description is a wall of comma-separated strings rather than an enum — inconsistent with other simdune schemas which use enum primitive. getActivityEVM hardcodes query params (limit, maxPages, requestDelay) in path instead of as parameters.
Briefly describes token holder distribution scope for ERC20/ERC721. Lacks scale or detail compared to siblings. Mentions ranking by wallet value.
Uses chain_id as numeric enum (less human-readable than chainName used in sibling schemas — inconsistency). Token address regex correct. Tests use well-known tokens (USDC, WETH) on multiple chains.
Describes SVM transaction history with block info and ordering. Brief but accurate. Could note specific data fields returned.

Base58 regex for SVM addresses, single-value enum (SOLANA) for chainName, limit range 1-1000. Output schema is minimal (transactions as array of strings) which is less informative than EVM counterpart.
Beschreibt klar die Datenquelle (deutscher Strommarkt von SMARD/Bundesnetzagentur) und Datentypen, bleibt aber relativ generisch ohne konkrete Use Cases.
Filter-IDs (1223/1224/1225/4169) sind direkt in den Endpoint-Beschreibungen dokumentiert mit lesbaren Labels. Tests demonstrieren typische Aufrufe. Defaults sind sinnvoll gesetzt.
Klare Beschreibung (ISRIC SoilGrids, 250m Aufloesung, WRB-Klassifikation, multiple Tiefen). Erkennbar fuer welche Use Cases nutzbar (Bodenkunde, Landwirtschaft).

lat/lon Bereiche korrekt validiert, gute Test-Cases mit globalen Locations. Properties/depth/value-Listen sind als generische array() typisiert ohne enum-Optionen — LLM muss raten welche Werte gueltig sind (clay/sand/phh2o etc. nur im Description-Text).
Klar (top-profitable Traders, Pagination, optional token-specific). Knapp aber praezise.
Endpoint-Schema vermutlich klein mit Pagination-Params und optionaler Token-Adresse. Tests sollten dies abdecken.
Klar (SolSniffer Token-Security-Analyse, Risk-Assessment, Rug-Pull, Mint Authority, Safety Score). Konkrete Anwendung erkennbar.
token_address als string min(32) plausibel fuer Solana, ein Test-Beispiel (USDC). Output zeigt score/risks/tokenData generisch — LLM bekommt wenig Hinweis was im risks-Array steht.
Klar aber knapp (Sourcify, Smart-Contract Source Verification, multi-chain). Use Cases (verification status) erkennbar.
chainId/address Parameter sauber validiert (length(42) fuer Adresse, min(1) fuer chainId), Test-Beispiel mit USDC Ethereum, mehrere Endpoints differenziert (Status, checkByAddresses, SourceFileTree).
Praezise (SPACE ID on-chain Resolution fuer Domains und Adressen). Kompakt aber klar.

getSupportedChains liefert Lookup-Tabelle, getAddress/getName sind klar dokumentiert. Tests mit konkretem Sei-Beispiel (steven.sei). chainid als number — sollte ggf. enum sein damit LLM gueltige IDs kennt.
Sehr praezise (BfS Strahlenschutz, ambient dose rate, WFS/OGC, 1685 Stationen). Quelle und Datenmenge klar.

WFS-typische Parameter, maxFeatures als string optional, getStationTimeseries braucht viewparams-String im Format 'kenn:031020004' — gut beschrieben in Description, aber LLM muss erst getLatestReadings aufrufen um valide kenn-Werte zu finden.
Klar (ARD tagesschau, Headlines, Suche, Livestreams). Provider und Use Case klar erkennbar.
Bei 6.2k Zeichen mehrere Endpoints, ARD-API ist typischerweise gut strukturiert mit Ressorts und Region-Filtern. Datenfelder sollten gut dokumentiert sein.
Description clearly states purpose (GraphQL schemas + arbitrary queries on The Graph subgraphs). Slightly brief but specific enough to know when to use.
Parameters are minimal; subgraphId and query are typed but lack format hints (e.g. ID encoding) — descriptions on tool level are okay but per-parameter descriptions missing.
Description is brief: price discovery via Uniswap V3 with CryptoCompare fallback. Adequate but lacks scope details (supported chains, fee tier, return format).

getTokenPrice documents fee tier (0.3%), chainAlias enum with default ETHEREUM_MAINNET, address parameter validated with min(1). Tests use real DAI/USDC addresses. Per-parameter description fields not used.
Description states Uniswap V2/V3/V4 pools via The Graph subgraphs, but is brief and lacks detail on data returned.

Tools document token_address/pool_id (length 42 validation), chain enum with 7 supported networks and default, concrete USDC test data. Mentions only V3 pools in description despite V2/V3/V4 claim — slight inconsistency.
Description is specific: city (Nuremberg/VAG), data type (real-time stops/departures/service info), modes (bus, tram, subway). Compact and informative.

Only one parameter (stopId) with a default value but no explanation of how to discover IDs. getStops has no parameters; getDepartures references getStops only indirectly. Output schema is detailed but German field names lack English notes.
Specific: provider (Wormholescan), domain (cross-chain bridge metrics), capabilities (activity, money flow, top assets/pairs/corridors, KPIs).

Enums for timeSpan with defaults are well-defined. However chain IDs (sourceChain, targetChain) are required as opaque strings without lookup table or examples. 'motional' in enum(notional,motional) appears to be a typo/error and is unexplained.
Beschreibung klar: ABI + Source ueber 60+ EVM-Chains. Erwaehnt Listen-Funktion und Lookup-by-Address. Kontext eindeutig.

Generische Tool-Beschreibungen (Copy-Paste-Stil 'Returns structured JSON response data'), Output-Schema ist sehr duenn ('result: string'). Enum-Interpolation via SharedList ist gut, aber Tool-Beschreibungen sind weniger spezifisch als bei Ethereum-Variante.
Clear scope: comprehensive NFT data via Moralis (stats, metadata, ownership, transfers, trades, traits, rankings) across 30+ EVM chains. Adequate routing info.

Boolean enums (false,true) and chain enum parameterized. Tool keys are raw paths, no semantic names. Many params lack descriptions (just types). No output schemas. Required vs optional indicated. Test addresses appear to be the same generic address rather than real NFT contracts.
Brief description naming DWD (Deutscher Wetterdienst) and WarnWetter API. Could specify what data is returned (forecasts, warnings), coverage, or freshness.

Single tool with default station (Berlin Tegel 10382). Tool description explains forecast1/forecast2 distinction and the handler simplification. Output schema is very detailed including unit conventions (1/10 degrees). However, parameter description for stationIds is missing.
Clear and concise: names the source (Cornell Lab), data types (recent/notable sightings, hotspots), and global scope. Could mention API key requirement.

Tool descriptions and tests show concrete region codes (US-NY, DE). Numeric constraints with defaults and max are clear. However, parameters lack individual descriptions, and regionCode format/source is only inferable from examples. Output schemas have field types but no descriptions.
Clear purpose (browse/search MCP server registry) but description is brief and does not detail filtering capabilities or scale. Adequate for routing decisions.

Pagination parameters well-bounded with defaults. However, listServers and searchServers expose identical params (no actual search/keyword param) despite description suggesting keyword search; output schema is rich but the searchServers tool lacks a search query parameter, which is misleading.
Clear scope: Medium articles via RSS, four entry points (tag, user, publication, topic). Mentions metadata/summaries. Adequate specificity.

Parameter descriptions are generic (Returns structured JSON response data). No output schemas defined. getTopicFeed has a useful enum of valid topics, but other tools lack examples of valid identifier formats (e.g. how to find publication slugs). Tests provide some guidance.
Kurze Beschreibung nennt x402 und Coinbase Bazaar, aber bleibt vage zu Inhalt, Filtern und konkretem Anwendungsfall.
Limit/Offset gut beschrieben mit Constraints; Output-Schema-Felder (accepts, metadata, resource) haben aber keine inhaltlichen Beschreibungen, nur Typen.
Klar: Datenquelle (Berlin WFS GeoJSON), Inhalt, Zugangsbedingung. Spezifisch genug fuer LLM-Routing. Use-Cases koennten klarer sein.
Beide Parameter haben klare Beschreibungen. Schema-Struktur ungewoehnlich (z mit options, description neben position). Wichtige Filter wie Bezirk/Schulart fehlen.
Beschreibung ist klar und nennt Datenarten, bleibt aber relativ kurz ohne konkrete Use-Cases oder Beispielanfragen.

Parameter haben Schluessel und Constraints (NCT-Regex, pageSize-Range), aber KEINE description-Felder pro Parameter; Tool-Description nennt Filter, aber Parameter selbst sind nicht einzeln dokumentiert.
Concise and specific: lists data types (FDA labels, SPL, NDC, drug classes) and the source (NLM DailyMed REST).

Tool descriptions mention cross-references and parameters have constraints (max(100), default), but individual parameters lack per-parameter description fields, leaving e.g. setid/ndc semantics implicit.
Description briefly mentions key leagues (Bundesliga, Premier League, La Liga) and data source (GitHub static JSON). Lacks scope details on coverage (history, granularity).

Single season parameter is simple. Season format (2024-25) only shown in tests, not in description. Repeated structure across many leagues with no format guidance for season string. Could be consolidated to a single league-selector tool.
Description identifies Passport scores/credentials/attestations and networks (Ethereum, Arbitrum) but is brief — could benefit from explaining 'Passport' (Gitcoin Passport sybil resistance) context.
Only one tool with one parameter (userAddress), constrained to exactly 42 chars (Ethereum address). Tests cover real/null cases. However, parameter lacks an explicit description string.
Description gives scale (160+ indicators), regional granularity (federal states, districts, Kreise), and topical coverage (population, demographics, economy, education, environment). Clear trigger.

The 'layer' parameter requires constructing a complex nested ArcGIS JSON query string with SQL embedded — extremely difficult for an LLM to produce correctly without explicit format guidance. listAvailableIndicators helps but the burden on the LLM is very high.
Identifies Solana scope and key capabilities (safety scores, risk reports, votes, trending). Source (rugcheck.xyz) noted. Lacks concrete metrics or counts.

Mint address validation via min(32). Cross-references between getTokenVotes and getTokenReport. Tool descriptions sometimes include filler ('Returns structured JSON response data'). Test coverage is minimal (one mint per tool).
Klar (Solscan, allgemeine Solana-Chain-Info: Block height, TX count, TPS, supply). Sehr eng gefasste, klar erkennbare Funktion.

Endpoint hat keine Parameter (chainInfo() returnt static info). Output-Schema dokumentiert blockHeight/currentEpoch/absoluteSlot/transactionCount. Einfach zu nutzen, aber sehr minimaler Funktionsumfang.
Klar (Stolpersteine in Berlin, Holocaust-Gedenkstaetten). Use Cases (Recherche, Bildung) implizit erkennbar.
Bei 5.5k Zeichen Schema-Length wahrscheinlich mehrere Endpoints (Liste, Filter). Berliner Open-Data-Endpoints typischerweise gut strukturiert, Filterparameter abhaengig von Datenfeldern.
Klar (SwaggerHub Registry-API, Search, Metadata, Swagger-Definitionen). Use Cases offensichtlich fuer API-Konsumenten.
Search-Parameter (query/state/page/limit) gut typisiert, state als Enum (PUBLISHED/UNPUBLISHED). page/limit als string statt number ist suboptimal — LLM koennte Number erwarten.
Specific provider (official ZVG portal of German courts) and domain (foreclosure auctions), but compact and lacks detail on coverage or available filters.

State codes enumerated as 16-value enum but without name mapping (codes only). Other enums (art, obj_arr, order_by) have raw integer values with no descriptions. Output schema is generic with status/data only. Tests demonstrate only land_abk.
